acceptanceCriteria: A versioned availability artifact represents each configured candidate as exactly one of available, unavailable, unknown, or incompatible.

Probing reads only documented, bounded interfaces for Claude Code model configuration and optional codex-plugin-cc presence; it performs no installation, no authentication, and no network transmission.
Tool or plugin version and observation time are recorded when observable and represented as explicitly unknown otherwise, never fabricated.
A candidate whose availability is unavailable or incompatible is filtered before attempt start; missing codex-plugin-cc excludes only candidates that require it.
Unsupported, failed, or format-changed probing is represented as unknown, not available, and the flow fails safely.
The router never silently substitutes a different model; an unroutable or unavailable selection stops with an explicit result.
Runtime model identity, reasoning effort, usage, and cost remain unverified/unknown; availability is never presented as runtime attestation.
Tests use fixtures or mocks and do not require live provider access.
Documentation clearly separates availability, configured identity, and attested identity.
Existing v1 artifacts remain readable; schema changes are additive.
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
